target.title: docs(plugins): document subagent_ended hook fields
target.body:
## Summary

Document the `subagent_ended` hook payload in `docs/plugins/hooks.md`. The Subagents section already listed the hook and detailed `subagent_spawned`, but `subagent_ended` had no field description, so plugin authors could reasonably assume it carried `agentId` like its sibling event. In reality `PluginHookSubagentEndedEvent` uses `targetSessionKey` as its identity field and does not include `agentId` or `childSessionKey`.

- Added one bullet describing `subagent_ended` fields: `targetSessionKey`, `targetKind`, `reason`, `outcome`, and the optional `error`, `runId`, `endedAt`, `accountId`, `sendFarewell` fields.
- Explicitly notes the absence of `agentId`/`childSessionKey` and recommends correlating via `targetSessionKey`.

Closes #95186

## Validation Evidence

Docs-only change. Verified the documented fields against `src/plugins/hook-types.ts` (`PluginHookSubagentEndedEvent`, lines 808–818) and `PluginHookSubagentSpawnBase` (lines 720–732) to confirm which fields are present vs. absent. No runtime code was changed.

## Security & Privacy Impact

- New permissions, capabilities, or file system access scope? `No`
- New external network calls? `No`
- Secrets / tokens / credentials handling changed? `No`
- PII, real identities, or personal data in diff, tests, fixtures, or docs? `No`

## Compatibility

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config / env / CLI surface changed? `No`
